sequence.speaker: Brad
text: There he is! The fisherman returns!
Brad was already pouring something before Abel had fully sat down.

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: Walsh sent me. Said you'd 'sort me out.'

sequence.speaker: Brad
text: Ha! That old salt. He says that about everyone. Here.
Brad slid a glass across the counter. It was filled considerably past what Abel would consider a normal pour.

sequence.speaker: Brad
text: First day on the water calls for a celebration. How'd it feel out there?

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: Exhausting. But... kind of meditative? I didn't think about anything except the fish.

sequence.speaker: Brad
text: That's the magic of it. Fishing demands your full attention. No room for all that other noise.
Brad refilled the glass without being asked.

sequence.speaker: Brad
text: Oh hey, speaking of fish- we've got the Loveland County Fish Boil coming up. Big event. Whole town comes out.

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: A fish boil? Like a... cookout?

sequence.speaker: Brad
text: The talk of all Maine! Well, the talk of this particular stretch of coast anyway. You'll see.

sequence.speaker: Brad
text: Best part is, you'll be eating your own catch. Nothing like it.
